name: Mrs. Fields Carrot Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Fresh lemon juice (about 1/2 large lemon)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Grease and flour two 9-inch cake pans.
3. In a large bowl, stir together flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and sugars.
4. Add butter, one egg, and vanilla; blend with an electric mixer on low speed.
5. Increase speed to medium and beat for 2 minutes.
6. Scrape down the sides of the bowl and add remaining eggs, one at a time, beating 30 seconds after each addition.
7. Add carrots, pineapple, raisins, and walnuts.
8. Blend until thoroughly combined.
9. Pour batter into prepared pans and smooth the surface with a rubber spatula.
10. Bake in the center of the oven for 60-70 minutes. The toothpick inserted into the center should come out clean.
11. Cool in the pans for 10 minutes, then invert cakes onto a rack and cool to room temperature.
1. In a medium bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed, beat cream cheese and butter until smooth.
2. Add lemon juice and vanilla; beat until combined.
3. Add sugar gradually, mixing on low until smooth.
4. To ice the carrot cake, place one layer on a cake platter and spread icing over the top to form a thin filling.
5. Place the second layer over the first, rounded side up. Coat the top and sides of the cake evenly with remaining icing.
6. Refrigerate 1 hour to set the icing.
